description: Issue #186: Improve PDF parser robustness for content stream errors

userStories.title: Add error recovery for unrecognized content stream operators
userStories.description: The content stream interpreter fails when encountering unrecognized operators, causing entire pages to produce 0% extraction. Implement graceful error recovery — skip unrecognized operators instead of aborting the page. Log warnings for skipped operators.
acceptanceCriteria: Unrecognized operators are skipped with a warning instead of aborting
issue-1054-example.pdf extracts chars > 50% (currently 0%, content stream operator gap)
No regression on PDFs with standard operators
Skipped operators are logged via tracing when the tracing feature is enabled
userStories.notes: Key file: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs. The interpreter's main loop matches operator names. Add a catch-all arm that logs and continues instead of returning an error. Check which specific operators cause failures in each affected PDF.

userStories.title: Fix annotation PDF parse failures
userStories.description: annotations.pdf and its rotated variants (90°, 180°, 270°) fail to parse entirely. Diagnose the specific parse error and fix it. These PDFs likely use annotation-related content stream operators or form XObjects that the parser doesn't handle.
acceptanceCriteria: Cross-validation entries upgraded from 'PDF parse error' to actual scores
userStories.notes: Annotation PDFs may use form XObjects (/Subtype /Form) for annotation appearances. The interpreter already handles /Do for XObjects but may not handle all form XObject subtypes. Also check: inline image operators (BI/ID/EI), type 3 font charprocs, or unusual resource dictionaries.

userStories.title: Fix remaining parse failures (issue-1181, issue-848)
userStories.description: issue-1181.pdf and issue-848.pdf fail to parse. Diagnose each failure, implement fixes or error recovery as appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: issue-1181.pdf opens without parse error (or gracefully degrades)
issue-848.pdf opens without parse error (or gracefully degrades)
Cross-validation entries updated with actual extraction scores
userStories.notes: These may be fixed by the error recovery from US-186-1. If not, diagnose each individually. Common causes: malformed cross-reference tables, unsupported encryption, corrupted streams.
